figures.caption: Figure 1: Illustration of our personalized social support task. Given the user’s profile and current utterance, the agent first selects and invokes appropriate tools, then generates a response based on the tool execution results.
Table 3: Comparison of preference alignment scores under the history-based setting.
Table 2: Performance comparison under the profile-based setting.
Figure 7: Nested pie chart showing the mapping between the 7 coarse-grained emotions and the 31 fine-grained emotions.
Table 8: Age-based constraints on educational attainment candidates during profile construction
Figure 6: Frequency distribution of the 31 fine-grained emotion categories.
Figure 4: A comparison between a tool-augmented response from ComPASS-Qwen and an empathetic response from GPT-5.1.

Table 1: List and taxonomy of the tools designed in this study. The 12 interactive tools span five multimedia application domains and were mapped to four social support types based on a questionnaire survey of psychology experts, with each tool assigned to up to two types.

Table 7: Social support type annotation for the tools. We report the results of a questionnaire survey with 5 psychology experts. Each “8” denotes one respondent who identified the type as the primary support type, and each “9” denotes one respondent who identified it as the secondary support type. Traditional empathetic response is also included as a separate behavior for comparison.
Table 6: Descriptions of the interactive tools in our social support environment.

Figure 2: Overview of the construction pipeline for ComPASS-Bench. We design a multi-step LLM-based synthesis process to generate user profiles, situations, and user utterances. Additional verification and human refinement steps are employed to ensure the quality of the final data. Note that all demographics in our dataset are fictitious, and no real personal data is used.
Figure 3: Statistics of our test set. (a) illustrates the age distribution of users, while (b) presents the distribution of the coarse-grained emotion categories associated with all situations.
